# Language validation status

<!-- Generated by tools/generate-language-status.py; do not edit. -->

Mark bundles **256 supported public language IDs**. **256 are validated** by the currently machine-readable contract below; **0 more are supported** by real bundled grammars and the catalog-wide smoke/budget gate.

For this ledger, *validated* means `cases.toml` contains both `basic` and `stress` source/golden pairs (10–30 and 140–260 source lines respectively), every golden record has `stoppedEarly: false`, and neither fixture uses a `divergences.toml` exception. The Rust harness checks both exact scope stacks and coarse syntax classes, and the persisted process-cold measurement must pass the policy floor. A `smoke` fixture alone is oracle coverage, not validation to this contract.

The independent `benchmarks/textmate/validation-policy.json` hard-locks expected public, validated, oracle, and stress-corpus counts at 256 each. Generation fails before writing this ledger if any count falls below that completed state.

The oracle manifest currently covers **256** public IDs. `catalog-repeated` contains the **256** IDs with stress fixtures. Every sweep member is locally gated at **≥ 2 MB/s** (run `check-textmate-catalog-performance.py` on a reference machine; not CI). The persisted sweep measures **12.07 MB/s aggregate**. All 256 promotion dates are the actual final-batch date, 2026-07-14; they are explicitly recorded per language in `benchmarks/textmate/language-promotions.json`, not inferred during generation. The strict contract was introduced on 2026-07-12.

Regenerate with `python3 tools/generate-language-status.py`; verify with `python3 tools/generate-language-status.py --check`. Performance numbers only change when `python3 tools/check-textmate-catalog-performance.py --write-report` is explicitly run.
